title: Simple Outdoor Dining Bench
summary: A basic 2x4-framed outdoor bench with 1x4 slats — the companion bench to a simple outdoor dining table, built from one shopping list per bench.
description:
This is the simplest bench in the catalog on purpose — a 2x4 frame, four 2x4 legs, and twelve 1x4 slats laid across the top with even gaps. It's sized and priced to sit alongside a simple outdoor dining table without becoming a bigger project than the table itself.

Build the frame first and check it for square before the legs go on, by measuring both diagonals and adjusting until they match — a frame that's slightly off-square at this stage stays off-square for the life of the bench. Attach the legs to the square frame, then lay the slats on top with a consistent 1/2" gap between them, which is enough for water to drain through without looking sparse.

The shopping list here is written per single bench — for a table that seats more people, just build as many benches as you need from the same cut list.

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Build the basic 2x4 frame with the frame ends and sides and the longer 2-1/2" screws. Check the frame for square by measuring both diagonals and adjusting until they match before moving on.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Screw the four legs to the square frame with the longer screws.

steps.title: Add the slats and finish
steps.body: Lay the slats out on top of the frame with a 1/2" gap between each one and screw down with the shorter screws. Sand and finish before use outdoors.

images.alt: Simple outdoor dining bench with a 2x4 frame and slatted top
